```{r}
           warp_mise_oracle > 0 & warp_mise_estimated > 0) |>
  mutate(log2_ratio = log2(warp_mise_estimated / warp_mise_oracle))

noise_degrad <- paired_by_noise |>
  group_by(method, noise_sd) |>
  summarise(
    median_ratio = median(2^log2_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    q25 = quantile(2^log2_ratio, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    q75 = quantile(2^log2_ratio, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    method_f = factor(method, levels = names(method_labels), labels = method_labels),
    noise_f = factor(noise_sd, labels = paste0("noise = ", c(0, 0.1)))
  )

ggplot(noise_degrad, aes(x = method_f, y = median_ratio, fill = noise_f)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.8), width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = q25, ymax = q75),
                position = position_dodge(0.8), width = 0.25) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#a6bddb", "#2b8cbe"), name = NULL) +
  labs(title = "Template Estimation Penalty by Noise Level",
       subtitle = "Median degradation ratio across DGPs (IQR error bars)",
       x = NULL, y = "Degradation ratio (estimated / oracle)") +
  theme_bench() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 40, hjust = 1))
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-study2-noise-interaction
#| fig-cap: "Per-DGP interaction: degradation ratio at noise=0 vs noise=0.1. Points above the diagonal = noise amplifies template estimation penalty."
#| fig-height: 7
#| fig-width: 10

# Compute per (dgp, method) degradation ratio at each noise level
degrad_by_noise_dgp <- paired_by_noise |>
  group_by(dgp, method, noise_sd) |>
  summarise(
    geom_mean_ratio = exp(mean(log(warp_mise_estimated / warp_mise_oracle), na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

degrad_wide <- degrad_by_noise_dgp |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = noise_sd, values_from = geom_mean_ratio,
              names_prefix = "noise_")

if ("noise_0" %in% names(degrad_wide) && "noise_0.1" %in% names(degrad_wide)) {
  degrad_wide <- degrad_wide |>
    mutate(
      method_f = factor(method, levels = names(method_labels), labels = method_labels),
      dgp_short = sub(":.*", "", dgp_labels[dgp])
    )

  lim_range <- range(c(degrad_wide$noise_0, degrad_wide$`noise_0.1`), na.rm = TRUE)
  lim_range <- c(min(0.5, lim_range[1]), max(4, lim_range[2]))

  ggplot(degrad_wide, aes(x = noise_0, y = `noise_0.1`, color = method)) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
    geom_text(aes(label = dgp_short), size = 2.2, vjust = -0.8, show.legend = FALSE) +
    scale_color_manual(values = method_colors, labels = method_labels) +
    scale_x_log10(limits = lim_range) +
    scale_y_log10(limits = lim_range) +
    labs(title = "Noise x Template Estimation Interaction",
         subtitle = "Points above diagonal: noise amplifies template estimation penalty",
         x = "Degradation ratio at noise = 0 (log scale)",
         y = "Degradation ratio at noise = 0.1 (log scale)",
         color = "Method") +
    theme_bench()
} else {
  cat("Insufficient noise levels for interaction plot.\n")
}
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-study2-noise-sensitivity
#| tbl-cap: "Degradation ratio by method and noise level. Ratio > 1 = worse with estimated template. Noise amplification = how much worse the penalty gets from clean to noisy data."

noise_summary <- paired_by_noise |>
  group_by(method, noise_sd) |>
  summarise(
    geom_mean = exp(mean(log(warp_mise_estimated / warp_mise_oracle), na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = noise_sd, values_from = geom_mean, names_prefix = "noise_")

if ("noise_0" %in% names(noise_summary) && "noise_0.1" %in% names(noise_summary)) {
  noise_summary <- noise_summary |>
    mutate(
      amplification = `noise_0.1` / noise_0,
      method_f = method_labels[method]
    ) |>
    arrange(amplification)

  noise_summary |>
    select(method_f, noise_0, `noise_0.1`, amplification) |>
    gt() |>
    tab_header(
      title = "Template Estimation Penalty: Noise Dependence",
      subtitle = "Geometric mean degradation ratio (estimated/oracle) per noise level"
    ) |>
    cols_label(method_f = "Method", noise_0 = "Clean (noise=0)",
               `noise_0.1` = "Noisy (noise=0.1)",
               amplification = "Noise amplification") |>
    fmt_number(columns = c(noise_0, `noise_0.1`, amplification), decimals = 2) |>
    tab_style(
      style = cell_fill(color = "#f8d7da"),
      locations = cells_body(columns = amplification,
                             rows = amplification > 1.5)
    ) |>
    tab_style(
      style = cell_fill(color = "#d4edda"),
      locations = cells_body(columns = amplification,
                             rows = amplification < 1.1)
    )
} else {
  cat("Insufficient noise levels for summary table.\n")
}
```

Methods with "noise amplification" close to 1 are equally penalized regardless of noise — the template estimation penalty is intrinsic to the algorithm, not noise-driven. Methods with amplification > 1.5 are more fragile: their template estimation quality degrades further when noise obscures the underlying signal.

## Timing

Template estimation adds computational overhead — particularly for SRVF, which computes the Karcher mean via iterative alignment. The bar chart compares median time per curve between studies, and the table below quantifies the ratio.

```{r}
#| label: fig-timing-comparison
#| fig-cap: "Median time per curve: oracle vs estimated template."
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-width: 9

time_long <- rbind(
  data.frame(
    method = paired_no_fail$method,
    time_per_curve = paired_no_fail$time_oracle / paired_no_fail$n_curves,
    study = "Oracle template"
  ),
  data.frame(
    method = paired_no_fail$method,
    time_per_curve = paired_no_fail$time_estimated / paired_no_fail$n_curves,
    study = "Estimated template"
  )
)
time_long <- time_long[
  time_long$method %in% template_methods & !is.na(time_long$time_per_curve), ]

time_summ <- time_long |>
  group_by(method, study) |>
  summarise(median_time = median(time_per_curve), .groups = "drop")
time_summ$method_f <- factor(time_summ$method, levels = names(method_labels),
                             labels = method_labels)
time_summ$study <- factor(time_summ$study,
                          levels = c("Oracle template", "Estimated template"))

ggplot(time_summ, aes(x = method_f, y = median_time, fill = study)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Oracle template" = "#a6bddb",
                               "Estimated template" = "#2b8cbe"),
                    name = NULL) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Computation Time: Oracle vs Estimated Template",
       x = NULL, y = "Median time (seconds / curve)") +
  theme_bench()
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-timing-ratio
#| tbl-cap: "Time ratio (estimated/oracle) per method."

time_ratio <- time_summ |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = study, values_from = median_time) |>
  mutate(ratio = `Estimated template` / `Oracle template`) |>
  select(method_f, `Oracle template`, `Estimated template`, ratio) |>
  arrange(desc(ratio))

time_ratio |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = "Time Overhead of Template Estimation") |>
  cols_label(method_f = "Method", `Oracle template` = "Oracle (s/curve)",
             `Estimated template` = "Estimated (s/curve)", ratio = "Ratio") |>
  fmt_number(columns = c(`Oracle template`, `Estimated template`), decimals = 4) |>
  fmt_number(columns = ratio, decimals = 1)
```

## Diagnostics

```{r}
#| label: tbl-study2-failures
#| tbl-cap: "Failure rate comparison: Study 1 (oracle) vs Study 2 (estimated template)."

pairable_dgps <- sort(unique(results_paired$dgp))

s1_fail <- results |>
  filter(dgp %in% pairable_dgps, method %in% template_methods) |>
  group_by(dgp, method) |>
  summarise(s1_rate = mean(failure) * 100, .groups = "drop")

s2_fail <- results_notemplate |>
  filter(dgp %in% pairable_dgps, method %in% template_methods) |>
  group_by(dgp, method) |>
  summarise(s2_rate = mean(failure) * 100, .groups = "drop")

fail_comp <- merge(s1_fail, s2_fail, by = c("dgp", "method"))
fail_comp <- fail_comp[fail_comp$s1_rate > 0 | fail_comp$s2_rate > 0, ]

if (nrow(fail_comp) > 0) {
  fail_comp |>
    arrange(dgp, method) |>
    gt() |>
    tab_header(title = "Failure Rate Comparison (%)") |>
    cols_label(dgp = "DGP", method = "Method",
               s1_rate = "Study 1 (oracle)", s2_rate = "Study 2 (estimated)") |>
    fmt_number(columns = c(s1_rate, s2_rate), decimals = 1)
} else {
  cat("No failures in either study for the 5 template-using methods on pairable DGPs.\n")
}
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-rank-changes
#| tbl-cap: "Rank changes between Study 1 and Study 2."

s1_rank <- summarize_metric(
  results[results$dgp %in% pairable_dgps & results$method %in% template_methods &
          !results$failure, ],
  c("dgp", "method"), "warp_mise"
) |>
  group_by(dgp) |>
  mutate(rank_s1 = rank(mean, ties.method = "min")) |>
  ungroup() |>
  select(dgp, method, rank_s1)

s2_rank <- summarize_metric(
  results_notemplate[results_notemplate$dgp %in% pairable_dgps &
                     results_notemplate$method %in% template_methods &
                     !results_notemplate$failure, ],
  c("dgp", "method"), "warp_mise"
) |>
  group_by(dgp) |>
  mutate(rank_s2 = rank(mean, ties.method = "min")) |>
  ungroup() |>
  select(dgp, method, rank_s2)

rank_change <- merge(s1_rank, s2_rank) |>
  mutate(change = rank_s2 - rank_s1) |>
  filter(abs(change) >= 2)

if (nrow(rank_change) > 0) {
  rank_change |>
    mutate(method_label = method_labels[method]) |>
    select(dgp, method_label, rank_s1, rank_s2, change) |>
    arrange(desc(abs(change))) |>
    gt() |>
    tab_header(title = "Rank Changes >= 2 Positions",
               subtitle = "Methods whose relative performance shifted with template estimation") |>
    cols_label(dgp = "DGP", method_label = "Method",
               rank_s1 = "Rank (Oracle)", rank_s2 = "Rank (Estimated)",
               change = "Change")
} else {
  cat("No method changed rank by 2 or more positions between studies.\n")
}
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-landmark-sanity
#| tbl-cap: "Landmark auto: sanity check (identical results across studies)."

# landmark_auto doesn't use template, so results should be identical
lm_paired <- merge(
  results[results$method == "landmark_auto",
          c(join_keys, "warp_mise")],
  results_notemplate[results_notemplate$method == "landmark_auto",
                     c(join_keys, "warp_mise")],
  by = join_keys, suffixes = c("_oracle", "_estimated")
)

if (nrow(lm_paired) > 0) {
  lm_paired$diff <- abs(lm_paired$warp_mise_oracle - lm_paired$warp_mise_estimated)
  max_diff <- max(lm_paired$diff, na.rm = TRUE)
  cat(sprintf("Landmark auto: %d paired reps, max |MISE difference| = %.2e\n",
              nrow(lm_paired), max_diff))
  if (max_diff < 1e-10) {
    cat("PASS: landmark_auto results are identical across studies (as expected).\n")
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("Note: small differences detected (max %.2e), likely due to numerical precision.\n",
                max_diff))
  }
} else {
  cat("No paired landmark_auto reps found.\n")
}
```

:::


# Study 3: Outlier Contamination {#sec-study3}

Studies 1–2 use clean data. Real data has outliers — curves with grossly wrong magnitude (amplitude outliers), severely misaligned features (phase outliers), or entirely wrong shape (shape outliers). Study 3 injects controlled contamination into four representative DGPs to answer: **which methods degrade gracefully under outlier contamination?**

```{r}
#| label: load-outlier
#| cache: false
outlier_file <- file.path(results_dir, "results_combined_outlier.rds")
if (file.exists(outlier_file)) {
  results_outlier <- readRDS(outlier_file)
  results_outlier$not_applicable <- (results_outlier$method == "landmark_oracle" &
                                       !results_outlier$dgp %in% c("S8", "S9"))
  cat(sprintf(
    "Study 3: %d rows | DGPs: %s | Outlier types: %s | Failure rate: %.1f%%\n",
    nrow(results_outlier),
    paste(sort(unique(results_outlier$dgp)), collapse = ", "),
    paste(sort(unique(results_outlier$outlier_type)), collapse = ", "),
    100 * mean(results_outlier$failure)
  ))
  has_outlier_results <- TRUE
} else {
  cat("Study 3 results not yet available.\n")
  has_outlier_results <- FALSE
}
```

```{r}
#| label: outlier-labels
#| cache: false
outlier_labels <- c(
  amplitude = "Amplitude (scale 3-5x)",
  phase = "Phase (severity 3.0)",
  shape = "Shape (wrong template)"
)
```

## Outlier Heatmap

```{r}
#| label: fig-outlier-heatmap
#| fig-cap: "Mean warp MISE under 10% contamination, by method, DGP, and outlier type. Color encodes log10(MISE). Compare with Study 1 clean-data baselines."
#| fig-height: 8
#| fig-width: 12
#| eval: !expr has_outlier_results

main_outlier <- results_outlier[
  results_outlier$contam_frac == 0.10 &
    !results_outlier$failure &
    !results_outlier$not_applicable, ]

heat_outlier <- summarize_metric(
  main_outlier,
  c("dgp", "method", "outlier_type"),
  "warp_mise"
)
heat_outlier <- heat_outlier[heat_outlier$method != "landmark_oracle", ]

heat_outlier$dgp <- factor(heat_outlier$dgp, levels = names(dgp_labels))
heat_outlier$method <- factor(heat_outlier$method,
                              levels = setdiff(names(method_labels), "landmark_oracle"))
heat_outlier$outlier_type <- factor(heat_outlier$outlier_type,
                                    levels = names(outlier_labels),
                                    labels = outlier_labels)

heat_outlier$log_mise <- log10(pmax(heat_outlier$mean, 1e-10))
mise_range <- range(heat_outlier$log_mise, na.rm = TRUE)
heat_outlier$text_color <- ifelse(
  scales::rescale(heat_outlier$log_mise, from = mise_range) > 0.6,
  "white", "black"
)

ggplot(heat_outlier, aes(x = method, y = dgp, fill = log_mise)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", log_mise), color = text_color),
            size = 2.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_identity() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "log10(MISE)", option = "plasma", direction = -1) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = method_labels) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = function(x) sub(":.*", "", dgp_labels[x])) +
  facet_wrap(~outlier_type, ncol = 1) +
  labs(title = "Warp MISE under 10% Contamination", x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_bench() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 40, hjust = 1))
```

## Degradation from Contamination

```{r}
#| label: fig-outlier-degradation
#| fig-cap: "Warp MISE ratio (contaminated / clean baseline from Study 1). Values > 1 indicate degradation. Faceted by outlier type."
#| fig-height: 7
#| fig-width: 12
#| eval: !expr has_outlier_results

# Get clean baselines from Study 1 (matched DGP, method, noise_sd=0.1, severity=0.5)
baseline <- results[
  !results$failure & !results$not_applicable &
    results$noise_sd == 0.1 & results$severity == 0.5, ]
baseline_summ <- summarize_metric(baseline, c("dgp", "method"), "warp_mise")
names(baseline_summ)[names(baseline_summ) == "mean"] <- "baseline_mise"

# Summarize outlier results (main arm only)
outlier_summ <- summarize_metric(
  main_outlier,
  c("dgp", "method", "outlier_type"),
  "warp_mise"
)

# Merge
degradation <- merge(
  outlier_summ,
  baseline_summ[, c("dgp", "method", "baseline_mise")],
  by = c("dgp", "method")
)
degradation$ratio <- degradation$mean / degradation$baseline_mise
degradation$log2_ratio <- log2(degradation$ratio)

degradation$method <- factor(degradation$method, levels = names(method_labels))
degradation$dgp <- factor(degradation$dgp, levels = names(dgp_labels))
degradation$outlier_type <- factor(degradation$outlier_type,
                                   levels = names(outlier_labels),
                                   labels = outlier_labels)

ggplot(degradation, aes(x = method, y = log2_ratio, fill = method)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  facet_grid(dgp ~ outlier_type,
             labeller = labeller(dgp = function(x) sub(":.*", "", dgp_labels[x]))) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = method_colors) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = method_labels) +
  labs(title = "Contamination Degradation Ratio",
       subtitle = "log2(contaminated MISE / clean MISE). 0 = no change, 1 = 2x worse",
       x = NULL, y = "log2(ratio)") +
  theme_bench() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 7))
```

## Dose-Response (S4, Amplitude Outliers)

```{r}
#| label: fig-outlier-dose
#| fig-cap: "Warp MISE vs contamination fraction for S4 with amplitude outliers. Shows how methods degrade as outlier proportion increases."
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-width: 9
#| eval: !expr has_outlier_results

dose_data <- results_outlier[
  results_outlier$dgp == "S4" &
    results_outlier$outlier_type == "amplitude" &
    !results_outlier$failure, ]

dose_summ <- summarize_metric(
  dose_data,
  c("method", "contam_frac"),
  "warp_mise"
)

# Add clean baseline (contam_frac = 0)
s4_baseline <- summarize_metric(
  results[results$dgp == "S4" & results$noise_sd == 0.1 &
            results$severity == 0.5 & !results$failure, ],
  "method",
  "warp_mise"
)
s4_baseline$contam_frac <- 0

dose_combined <- rbind(
  dose_summ[, c("method", "contam_frac", "mean", "se")],
  s4_baseline[, c("method", "contam_frac", "mean", "se")]
)
dose_combined$method <- factor(dose_combined$method, levels = names(method_labels))

ggplot(dose_combined, aes(x = contam_frac, y = mean, color = method)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean - 2 * se, ymax = mean + 2 * se), width = 0.01) +
  scale_color_manual(values = method_colors, labels = method_labels) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0, 0.05, 0.10, 0.20),
                     labels = c("0%", "5%", "10%", "20%")) +
  labs(title = "Dose-Response: S4 with Amplitude Outliers",
       x = "Contamination fraction", y = "Mean warp MISE",
       color = "Method") +
  theme_bench()
```

## Robustness Ranking

```{r}
#| label: tbl-outlier-robustness
#| tbl-cap: "Method robustness ranking: mean degradation ratio across DGPs and outlier types. Lower ratio = more robust."
#| eval: !expr has_outlier_results

robustness <- degradation[
  degradation$method %in% setdiff(names(method_labels), "landmark_oracle"), ]
robustness_ranking <- aggregate(
  cbind(ratio, baseline_mise) ~ method, data = robustness,
  FUN = median
)
robustness_ranking <- robustness_ranking[order(robustness_ranking$ratio), ]
robustness_ranking$rank <- seq_len(nrow(robustness_ranking))

robustness_ranking |>
  mutate(method_label = method_labels[as.character(method)]) |>
  select(rank, method_label, baseline_mise, ratio) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = "Outlier Robustness Ranking",
             subtitle = "Median degradation ratio (contaminated/clean) across all DGPs and outlier types") |>
  cols_label(rank = "Rank", method_label = "Method",
             baseline_mise = "Baseline MISE", ratio = "Median ratio") |>
  fmt_number(columns = c(baseline_mise, ratio), decimals = 4) |>
  tab_style(
    style = cell_fill(color = "#d4edda"),
    locations = cells_body(rows = rank <= 2)
  )
```

## Case Study: Outlier Effects on Warp Recovery

```{r}
#| label: fig-casestudy-outlier
#| fig-cap: "Case study on S4 with 10% shape outliers (worst outlier type): SRVF warps are visibly distorted while landmark_auto is essentially unaffected. Red curves = outliers. Top: observed curves and true warps. Per-method rows: estimated warps and aligned curves."
#| fig-height: 12
#| fig-width: 10
#| eval: !expr has_outlier_results

# Generate clean S4 dataset, then contaminate with shape outliers
data_s4 <- generate_data("S4", n = 30, n_grid = 101, severity = 0.5,
                         noise_sd = 0, seed = 42)
data_s4c <- contaminate_data(data_s4, contam_frac = 0.10,
                             outlier_type = "shape", seed = 42)

fit_s4_srvf <- fit_method(data_s4c, "srvf")
fit_s4_lm <- fit_method(data_s4c, "landmark_auto")
fit_s4_fda <- fit_method(data_s4c, "fda_default")

plot_case_study(
  data_s4c,
  fits = list(SRVF = fit_s4_srvf,
              `Landmark (auto)` = fit_s4_lm,
              `FDA (default)` = fit_s4_fda),
  title = "S4 + 10% Shape Outliers: Warp Recovery Comparison",
  highlight_idx = which(data_s4c$outlier_mask)
)
```

The shape outliers (red curves) have entirely wrong template shapes, creating strong interference for methods that rely on global curve similarity. SRVF's Karcher mean is pulled toward the outliers, distorting all estimated warps. `landmark_auto` is largely unaffected because it relies on local extrema rather than global shape similarity, making it robust to shape contamination (consistent with its 1.06x aggregate degradation ratio). `fda_default` shows moderate degradation — its iterative alignment partially accommodates outliers.


# Study 4: Real-Data Templates {#sec-study4}

Studies 1–2 use two synthetic templates (sigbump, harmonic). Real functional data has different feature structures. Study 4 uses a **semi-synthetic approach**: extract templates from tf's bundled datasets (`pinch`, `growth`, `gait`), overlay synthetic warps, amplitude variation, and noise. Truth is still known, allowing rigorous evaluation. All real-data DGPs include realistic amplitude variation (`amp_sd = 0.5` vs 0.3 default) because real data always has inter-subject variability.

```{r}
#| label: load-realdata
#| cache: false
realdata_file <- file.path(results_dir, "results_combined_realdata.rds")
if (file.exists(realdata_file)) {
  results_realdata <- readRDS(realdata_file)
  results_realdata$not_applicable <- (results_realdata$method == "landmark_oracle" &
                                        !results_realdata$dgp %in% c("R3b"))
  cat(sprintf(
    "Study 4: %d rows | DGPs: %s | Failure rate: %.1f%%\n",
    nrow(results_realdata),
    paste(sort(unique(results_realdata$dgp)), collapse = ", "),
    100 * mean(results_realdata$failure)
  ))
  has_realdata_results <- TRUE
} else {
  cat("Study 4 results not yet available.\n")
  has_realdata_results <- FALSE
}
```

```{r}
#| label: realdata-labels
#| cache: false
rdgp_labels <- c(
  R1a = "R1a: pinch/smooth/rank2",
  R1b = "R1b: pinch/shift/mult",
  R2a = "R2a: growth-vel/smooth/rank2",
  R2b = "R2b: growth-vel/smooth/corr(orth)",
  R3a = "R3a: gait-hip/smooth/rank2",
  R3b = "R3b: gait-hip/landmark/mult"
)
```

## Template Showcase

```{r}
#| label: fig-realdata-templates
#| fig-cap: "Real-data templates extracted from tf's bundled datasets (pinch, growth velocity, gait hip angle), rescaled to [0,1] and z-scored."
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-width: 12

source(file.path(base_dir, "sim-dgp.R"))
arg <- seq(0, 1, length.out = 101)

templates_rd <- data.frame()
for (src in c("pinch", "growth_velocity", "gait_hip")) {
  tmpl <- make_template_from_data(src, arg)
  vals <- tf_evaluations(tmpl)[[1]]
  templates_rd <- rbind(templates_rd, data.frame(
    source = src, arg = arg, value = vals
  ))
}
templates_rd$source <- factor(templates_rd$source,
  levels = c("pinch", "growth_velocity", "gait_hip"),
  labels = c("Pinch (bell curve)", "Growth velocity (pubertal peak)", "Gait hip angle (oscillating)")
)

ggplot(templates_rd, aes(x = arg, y = value)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1, color = "#1b9e77") +
  facet_wrap(~source, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "Real-Data Templates", x = "t (rescaled to [0,1])", y = "z-scored value") +
  theme_bench()
```

## DGP Showcase

```{r}
#| label: fig-realdata-dgps
#| fig-cap: "Example draws from each real-data DGP (n=20, severity=0.5, noise=0). Blue: observed curves. Dashed: template."
#| fig-height: 10
#| fig-width: 12

# Generate one example per real-data DGP
dgps_rd <- c("R1a", "R1b", "R2a", "R2b", "R3a", "R3b")
plots_rd <- list()
for (dgp in dgps_rd) {
  d <- generate_data(dgp, n = 20, n_grid = 101, severity = 0.5, noise_sd = 0, seed = 123)
  x_mat <- as.matrix(d$x)
  t_vals <- tf_evaluations(d$template)[[1]]
  df_curves <- data.frame(
    arg = rep(arg, nrow(x_mat)),
    value = as.vector(t(x_mat)),
    id = rep(seq_len(nrow(x_mat)), each = length(arg))
  )
  df_template <- data.frame(arg = arg, value = t_vals)

  plots_rd[[dgp]] <- ggplot(df_curves, aes(x = arg, y = value, group = id)) +
    geom_line(alpha = 0.3, color = "steelblue") +
    geom_line(data = df_template, aes(group = NULL), linetype = "dashed",
              linewidth = 1, color = "black") +
    labs(title = rdgp_labels[dgp], x = "t", y = "x(t)") +
    theme_bench(base_size = 9)
}

patchwork::wrap_plots(plots_rd, ncol = 3)
```

## Heatmap

```{r}
#| label: fig-realdata-heatmap
#| fig-cap: "Mean warp MISE by method and real-data DGP (severity=0.5), stratified by noise level. Color encodes log10(MISE)."
#| fig-height: 8
#| fig-width: 10
#| eval: !expr has_realdata_results

rd_main <- results_realdata[
  results_realdata$severity == 0.5 &
    !results_realdata$failure &
    !results_realdata$not_applicable, ]

# Pooled summary (used for downstream rankings + synthetic comparison)
heat_rd <- summarize_metric(rd_main, c("dgp", "method"), "warp_mise")
heat_rd <- heat_rd[heat_rd$method != "landmark_oracle", ]
heat_rd$dgp <- factor(heat_rd$dgp, levels = names(rdgp_labels))
heat_rd$method <- factor(heat_rd$method,
                         levels = setdiff(names(method_labels), "landmark_oracle"))

# Noise-stratified summary for heatmap
heat_rd_noise <- summarize_metric(
  rd_main, c("dgp", "method", "noise_sd"), "warp_mise"
)
heat_rd_noise <- heat_rd_noise[heat_rd_noise$method != "landmark_oracle", ]
heat_rd_noise$dgp <- factor(heat_rd_noise$dgp, levels = names(rdgp_labels))
heat_rd_noise$method <- factor(heat_rd_noise$method,
                               levels = setdiff(names(method_labels), "landmark_oracle"))
heat_rd_noise$noise_label <- paste0("noise_sd = ", heat_rd_noise$noise_sd)

heat_rd_noise$log_mise <- log10(pmax(heat_rd_noise$mean, 1e-10))
mise_range <- range(heat_rd_noise$log_mise, na.rm = TRUE)
heat_rd_noise$text_color <- ifelse(
  scales::rescale(heat_rd_noise$log_mise, from = mise_range) > 0.6,
  "white", "black"
)

ggplot(heat_rd_noise, aes(x = method, y = dgp, fill = log_mise)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", log_mise), color = text_color),
            size = 3, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_identity() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "log10(MISE)", option = "plasma", direction = -1) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = method_labels) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = rdgp_labels) +
  facet_wrap(~noise_label) +
  labs(title = "Warp MISE: Real-Data DGPs", x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_bench() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 40, hjust = 1))
```

## Boxplots

```{r}
#| label: fig-realdata-boxplots
#| fig-cap: "Warp MISE distributions per method, faceted by real-data DGP. Log scale."
#| fig-height: 10
#| fig-width: 12
#| eval: !expr has_realdata_results

rd_box <- results_realdata[
  results_realdata$severity == 0.5 &
    !results_realdata$failure &
    !results_realdata$not_applicable &
    results_realdata$warp_mise > 0, ]
rd_box$dgp <- factor(rd_box$dgp, levels = names(rdgp_labels))
rd_box$method <- factor(rd_box$method, levels = names(method_labels))

ggplot(rd_box, aes(x = method, y = warp_mise, fill = method)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~dgp, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2,
             labeller = labeller(dgp = rdgp_labels)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = method_colors) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = method_labels) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(title = "Warp MISE Distributions: Real-Data DGPs", x = NULL, y = "Warp MISE (log scale)") +
  theme_bench() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 7))
```

## Rankings

```{r}
#| label: tbl-realdata-rankings
#| tbl-cap: "Method rankings by real-data DGP. Rank 1 = best (lowest mean warp MISE)."
#| eval: !expr has_realdata_results

rd_rankings <- heat_rd |>
  group_by(dgp) |>
  mutate(rank = rank(mean, ties.method = "min")) |>
  ungroup() |>
  select(dgp, method, mean, rank) |>
  mutate(method_label = method_labels[as.character(method)])

rd_rank_wide <- rd_rankings |>
  select(dgp, method_label, rank) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = dgp, values_from = rank)

rd_rank_wide |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = "Method Rankings: Real-Data DGPs") |>
  cols_label(method_label = "Method") |>
  data_color(
    columns = -method_label,
    palette = c("#d4edda", "#fff3cd", "#f8d7da"),
    domain = c(1, 6)
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-realdata-rankings-by-noise
#| tbl-cap: "Method rankings by noise level on real-data DGPs. Delta = rank change when adding noise (positive = rank worsens)."
#| eval: !expr has_realdata_results

rd_rank_noise <- heat_rd_noise |>
  group_by(noise_sd, dgp) |>
  mutate(rank = rank(mean, ties.method = "min")) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(method_label = method_labels[as.character(method)])

# Average rank per method at each noise level
rd_avg_rank <- rd_rank_noise |>
  group_by(method_label, noise_sd) |>
  summarise(avg_rank = mean(rank), .groups = "drop") |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = noise_sd, values_from = avg_rank,
              names_prefix = "noise_")

rd_avg_rank$delta <- rd_avg_rank$noise_0.1 - rd_avg_rank$noise_0
rd_avg_rank <- rd_avg_rank[order(rd_avg_rank$noise_0), ]

rd_avg_rank |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = "Average Rank by Noise Level (Real-Data DGPs)") |>
  cols_label(
    method_label = "Method",
    noise_0 = "Clean (noise=0)",
    noise_0.1 = "Noisy (noise=0.1)",
    delta = "\u0394 Rank"
  ) |>
  fmt_number(columns = c(noise_0, noise_0.1, delta), decimals = 1) |>
  data_color(
    columns = delta,
    palette = c("#d4edda", "white", "#f8d7da"),
    domain = c(-2, 2)
  )
```

## Case Study: Growth Velocity Registration

```{r}
#| label: fig-casestudy-realdata
#| fig-cap: "Case study on R2a (growth velocity template, smooth warps): SRVF captures smooth warp structure that affine cannot. Top: observed curves and true warps. Per-method rows: estimated warps and aligned curves."
#| fig-height: 10
#| fig-width: 10
#| eval: !expr has_realdata_results

# Generate one R2a dataset and fit three methods
data_r2a <- generate_data("R2a", n = 20, n_grid = 101, severity = 0.5,
                          noise_sd = 0, seed = 123)

fit_r2a_srvf <- fit_method(data_r2a, "srvf")
fit_r2a_fda <- fit_method(data_r2a, "fda_default")
fit_r2a_aff <- fit_method(data_r2a, "affine_ss")

plot_case_study(
  data_r2a,
  fits = list(SRVF = fit_r2a_srvf,
              `FDA (default)` = fit_r2a_fda,
              `Affine (shift+scale)` = fit_r2a_aff),
  title = "R2a: Growth Velocity — Smooth Warp Recovery"
)
```

SRVF (rank 1 on R2a) recovers the smooth, non-linear warp structure accurately. The affine model, constrained to shift+scale, cannot represent the curvature in the true warps, leading to systematic alignment errors especially in the mid-domain. FDA (default) captures moderate warp structure but with more variability than SRVF.

## Synthetic vs Real-Data Comparison

Note: Both synthetic and real-data rankings below pool across noise levels (noise_sd = 0 and 0.1) within each DGP. This averages over noise conditions, which may mask noise-dependent rank changes. The mapping from real-data to synthetic DGPs is based on warp type (smooth, shift, landmark) — see code comments for the specific mapping.

```{r}
#| label: fig-realdata-vs-synthetic
#| fig-cap: "Method rankings on synthetic DGPs (Study 1) vs real-data DGPs (Study 4). Connected dots show rank changes. Note: rankings pool across noise levels."
#| fig-height: 6
#| fig-width: 10
#| eval: !expr has_realdata_results

# Get Study 1 rankings for matched DGP types
# Map: R1a/R1b → S4/S1 (smooth/shift), R2a/R2b → S5/S6b (smooth+mult/corr),
#       R3a → S4 (smooth), R3b → S8 (landmark)
synthetic_map <- c(R1a = "S4", R1b = "S1", R2a = "S5", R2b = "S6b", R3a = "S4", R3b = "S8")

synth_rankings <- summarize_metric(
  results[!results$failure & !results$not_applicable &
            results$noise_sd %in% c(0, 0.1) & results$severity == 0.5 &
            results$dgp %in% unique(synthetic_map), ],
  c("dgp", "method"),
  "warp_mise"
)
synth_rankings <- synth_rankings[synth_rankings$method != "landmark_oracle", ]
synth_rankings <- synth_rankings |>
  group_by(dgp) |>
  mutate(rank = rank(mean, ties.method = "min")) |>
  ungroup()

# Build comparison
comp_rows <- list()
for (rdgp in names(synthetic_map)) {
  sdgp <- synthetic_map[rdgp]
  # Real-data rank
  rd_r <- rd_rankings[rd_rankings$dgp == rdgp, c("method", "rank")]
  names(rd_r)[2] <- "rank_real"
  # Synthetic rank
  sy_r <- synth_rankings[synth_rankings$dgp == sdgp, c("method", "rank")]
  names(sy_r)[2] <- "rank_synth"
  m <- merge(rd_r, sy_r, by = "method")
  m$rdgp <- rdgp
  m$sdgp <- sdgp
  comp_rows[[rdgp]] <- m
}
comp_df <- do.call(rbind, comp_rows)
comp_df$method_label <- method_labels[as.character(comp_df$method)]
comp_df$rdgp <- factor(comp_df$rdgp, levels = names(rdgp_labels))

ggplot(comp_df, aes(y = method_label)) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = rank_synth, xend = rank_real, yend = method_label),
               color = "grey60", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(aes(x = rank_synth), color = "steelblue", size = 3) +
  geom_point(aes(x = rank_real), color = "coral", size = 3) +
  facet_wrap(~rdgp, ncol = 3, labeller = labeller(rdgp = rdgp_labels)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:6) +
  labs(title = "Synthetic vs Real-Data Rankings",
       subtitle = "Blue = synthetic DGP rank, Red = real-data DGP rank",
       x = "Rank (1 = best)", y = NULL) +
  theme_bench()
```


# Post-hoc Analysis {#sec-posthoc}

Deeper analysis of existing Studies 1–2 results (~36K runs). No new simulations needed.

## Pareto Fronts

For each DGP, methods trade off accuracy (warp MISE) against speed (computation time). Pareto-optimal methods are those where no other method is both faster and more accurate. These represent the "best available" choices at each accuracy-speed tradeoff point.

```{r}
#| label: fig-pareto
#| fig-cap: "Accuracy-speed Pareto fronts by DGP group. Each point is a method; x = median warp MISE, y = median time/curve, size = 1 - failure rate. Pareto-optimal methods are connected."
#| fig-height: 8
#| fig-width: 12

core_for_pareto <- results[
  results$grid_name == "core" &
    !results$not_applicable &
    results$severity == 0.5 &
    results$noise_sd %in% c(0, 0.05, 0.1), ]

pareto_data <- core_for_pareto |>
  group_by(dgp, method) |>
  summarise(
    med_mise = median(warp_mise, na.rm = TRUE),
    med_time = median(time / n_curves, na.rm = TRUE),
    failure_rate = mean(failure),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    reliability = 1 - failure_rate,
    method_label = method_labels[method]
  )

# Find Pareto front per DGP
find_pareto <- function(df) {
  df <- df[order(df$med_mise), ]
  pareto <- logical(nrow(df))
  min_time <- Inf
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(df))) {
    if (df$med_time[i] < min_time) {
      pareto[i] <- TRUE
      min_time <- df$med_time[i]
    }
  }
  df$pareto <- pareto
  df
}

pareto_data <- pareto_data |>
  group_by(dgp) |>
  group_modify(~find_pareto(.x)) |>
  ungroup()

# Assign DGP groups
pareto_data$dgp_group <- case_when(
  pareto_data$dgp %in% c("S1", "S2", "S11") ~ "Affine/Shift",
  pareto_data$dgp %in% c("S3", "S4", "S5", "S6a", "S6b", "S7", "S10") ~ "Smooth",
  pareto_data$dgp %in% c("S8", "S9") ~ "Landmark",
  TRUE ~ "Other"
)

ggplot(pareto_data, aes(x = med_mise, y = med_time)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = method, size = reliability), alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_line(data = pareto_data[pareto_data$pareto, ],
            aes(group = dgp), color = "grey40", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sub(":.*", "", dgp_labels[dgp])),
            size = 2, vjust = -1, color = "grey40") +
  facet_wrap(~dgp_group, scales = "free") +
  scale_color_manual(values = method_colors, labels = method_labels) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(1, 5), name = "Reliability") +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(title = "Accuracy-Speed Pareto Fronts",
       x = "Median warp MISE (log scale)", y = "Median time/curve (log scale, seconds)",
       color = "Method") +
  theme_bench()
```

## Pairwise Win Probabilities

For each method pair (A, B) and DGP, we compute the fraction of reps where A beats B on warp MISE. This gives a **tournament-style** view of method dominance, independent of the specific MISE values.

```{r}
#| label: fig-pairwise-wins
#| fig-cap: "Pairwise win probability heatmap (aggregated across core DGPs). Cell (A,B) = fraction of reps where method A has lower warp MISE than method B."
#| fig-height: 6
#| fig-width: 8

core_wide <- results[
  results$grid_name == "core" &
    !results$failure &
    !results$not_applicable &
    results$method != "landmark_oracle", ]

# For each (dgp, noise_sd, severity, rep), get per-method MISE
key_cols <- c("dgp", "noise_sd", "severity", "rep")
mise_wide <- core_wide |>
  select(all_of(c(key_cols, "method", "warp_mise"))) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = method, values_from = warp_mise)

methods_pw <- setdiff(names(method_labels), "landmark_oracle")
n_methods <- length(methods_pw)
win_matrix <- matrix(0, nrow = n_methods, ncol = n_methods,
                     dimnames = list(methods_pw, methods_pw))

for (i in seq_along(methods_pw)) {
  for (j in seq_along(methods_pw)) {
    if (i == j) {
      win_matrix[i, j] <- 0.5
      next
    }
    a <- mise_wide[[methods_pw[i]]]
    b <- mise_wide[[methods_pw[j]]]
    valid <- !is.na(a) & !is.na(b)
    if (sum(valid) == 0) next
    win_matrix[i, j] <- mean(a[valid] < b[valid])
  }
}

win_df <- as.data.frame(as.table(win_matrix))
names(win_df) <- c("method_a", "method_b", "win_prob")
win_df$method_a <- factor(win_df$method_a, levels = methods_pw)
win_df$method_b <- factor(win_df$method_b, levels = methods_pw)
win_df$text_color <- ifelse(win_df$win_prob > 0.6, "white", "black")

ggplot(win_df, aes(x = method_b, y = method_a, fill = win_prob)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.0f%%", 100 * win_prob), color = text_color),
            size = 3, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_identity() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#d95f02", mid = "white", high = "#1b9e77",
                       midpoint = 0.5, name = "Win probability",
                       labels = scales::percent) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = method_labels, position = "top") +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = method_labels) +
  labs(title = "Pairwise Win Probabilities",
       subtitle = "P(row method beats column method on warp MISE), averaged across core DGPs",
       x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_bench() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 40, hjust = 0))
```

## Ranking Stability

Bootstrap 95% CIs on method ranks per DGP. If rankings are stable, the CIs are narrow; if methods are closely matched, the CIs overlap. This answers: **how confident are we in the rankings?**

```{r}
#| label: fig-ranking-stability
#| fig-cap: "Bootstrap 95% CIs on method ranks per core DGP. Narrow intervals = confident ranking. Overlapping intervals = methods hard to distinguish."
#| fig-height: 10
#| fig-width: 10

set.seed(42)
n_boot <- 1000

core_rank_data <- results[
  results$grid_name == "core" &
    !results$failure &
    !results$not_applicable &
    results$method != "landmark_oracle" &
    results$severity == 0.5, ]

boot_ranks <- list()
for (d in unique(core_rank_data$dgp)) {
  dgp_data <- core_rank_data[core_rank_data$dgp == d, ]
  reps_avail <- unique(dgp_data$rep)
  n_reps <- length(reps_avail)

  rank_mat <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_boot, ncol = length(methods_pw),
                     dimnames = list(NULL, methods_pw))

  for (b in seq_len(n_boot)) {
    boot_reps <- sample(reps_avail, n_reps, replace = TRUE)
    boot_data <- dgp_data[dgp_data$rep %in% boot_reps, ]
    method_means <- tapply(boot_data$warp_mise, boot_data$method, mean, na.rm = TRUE)
    present <- intersect(names(method_means), methods_pw)
    ranks <- rank(method_means[present], ties.method = "average")
    rank_mat[b, present] <- ranks
  }

  for (m in methods_pw) {
    if (all(is.na(rank_mat[, m]))) next
    ci <- quantile(rank_mat[, m], c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
    boot_ranks[[length(boot_ranks) + 1]] <- data.frame(
      dgp = d, method = m,
      rank_median = median(rank_mat[, m], na.rm = TRUE),
      rank_lower = ci[1],
      rank_upper = ci[2]
    )
  }
}

boot_df <- do.call(rbind, boot_ranks)
boot_df$dgp <- factor(boot_df$dgp, levels = names(dgp_labels))
boot_df$method <- factor(boot_df$method, levels = methods_pw)

ggplot(boot_df, aes(x = rank_median, y = method, color = method)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = rank_lower, xmax = rank_upper), height = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~dgp, ncol = 2,
             labeller = labeller(dgp = function(x) sub(":.*", "", dgp_labels[x]))) +
  scale_color_manual(values = method_colors, labels = method_labels, guide = "none") +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = method_labels) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:6) +
  labs(title = "Ranking Stability (Bootstrap 95% CIs)",
       x = "Rank (1 = best)", y = NULL) +
  theme_bench()
```

## Failure Mode Diagnostics

```{r}
#| label: fig-failure-diagnostics
#| fig-cap: "Failure classification by method and DGP. Includes both hard failures (method errors) and soft failures (MISE > 10x median for that DGP)."
#| fig-height: 6
#| fig-width: 10

all_study1 <- results[!results$not_applicable, ]

# Hard failures
hard <- all_study1[all_study1$failure, ]
hard$failure_type <- "Hard (error)"

# Soft failures: non-failure but MISE > 10x median
success <- all_study1[!all_study1$failure, ]
dgp_medians <- tapply(success$warp_mise, success$dgp, median, na.rm = TRUE)
success$dgp_median <- dgp_medians[success$dgp]
soft <- success[!is.na(success$warp_mise) & success$warp_mise > 10 * success$dgp_median, ]
soft$failure_type <- "Soft (MISE > 10x median)"

failures_combined <- rbind(
  hard[, c("dgp", "method", "failure_type")],
  soft[, c("dgp", "method", "failure_type")]
)

if (nrow(failures_combined) > 0) {
  fail_counts <- failures_combined |>
    count(method, failure_type) |>
    mutate(method = factor(method, levels = names(method_labels)))

  ggplot(fail_counts, aes(x = method, y = n, fill = failure_type)) +
    geom_col(position = "stack") +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = method_labels) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Hard (error)" = "#e41a1c",
                                 "Soft (MISE > 10x median)" = "#ff7f00")) +
    labs(title = "Failure Mode Classification",
         x = NULL, y = "Count", fill = "Type") +
    theme_bench() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 40, hjust = 1))
} else {
  cat("No failures or soft failures detected.\n")
}
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-soft-failure-breakdown
#| tbl-cap: "Soft failure rates (MISE > 10x median) by method and DGP."

if (nrow(soft) > 0) {
  soft_rates <- soft |>
    count(dgp, method, name = "n_soft") |>
    left_join(
      all_study1 |>
        filter(!failure) |>
        count(dgp, method, name = "n_total"),
      by = c("dgp", "method")
    ) |>
    mutate(rate = n_soft / n_total)

  soft_rates |>
    mutate(method_label = method_labels[method]) |>
    select(dgp, method_label, n_soft, n_total, rate) |>
    arrange(desc(rate)) |>
    head(15) |>
    gt() |>
    tab_header(title = "Top 15 Soft Failure Rates") |>
    cols_label(dgp = "DGP", method_label = "Method", n_soft = "Soft failures",
               n_total = "Total runs", rate = "Rate") |>
    fmt_percent(columns = rate, decimals = 1)
} else {
  cat("No soft failures detected.\n")
}
```


# Reproducibility

```{r}
#| label: reproducibility
#| cache: false
cat("Seed strategy: digest::digest2int(paste(dgp, rep))\n")
cat("Parallel processing: parallel::mclapply (6 cores)\n")
cat("RNG: deterministic per-task seeds, independent across reps\n")
cat("Results directory:", results_dir, "\n")
cat("Study 1 results: results_combined_all.rds\n")
cat("Study 2 results: results_combined_notemplate.rds\n")
cat(sprintf("Study 1: %d runs | Failure rate: %.1f%%\n",
            nrow(results), 100 * mean(results$failure)))
cat(sprintf("Study 2: %d runs | Failure rate: %.1f%%\n",
            nrow(results_notemplate), 100 * mean(results_notemplate$failure)))
if (exists("results_outlier")) {
  cat(sprintf("Study 3: %d runs | Failure rate: %.1f%%\n",
              nrow(results_outlier), 100 * mean(results_outlier$failure)))
}
if (exists("results_realdata")) {
  cat(sprintf("Study 4: %d runs | Failure rate: %.1f%%\n",
              nrow(results_realdata), 100 * mean(results_realdata$failure)))
}
cat(sprintf("Total: %d runs\n",
            nrow(results) + nrow(results_notemplate) +
            (if (exists("results_outlier")) nrow(results_outlier) else 0) +
            (if (exists("results_realdata")) nrow(results_realdata) else 0)))
cat("\n")
sessionInfo()
```
